(32) Learning Hexo from Scratch - Extras: Updating Hexo
Updating matters, but updating Hexo is genuinely painful. So here's an extras post on how to update Hexo.
While I was writing the "Learning Hexo from Scratch" series, Hexo quietly moved from Hexo 4 to Hexo 5. So this post is going to skip straight from Hexo 3.9.0 to Hexo 5.2.0.
Earlier I mentioned that updating Hexo is a pain. How painful? Well — you basically rebuild your Hexo project from scratch.
I'd recommend updating your local Hexo CLI first (current version is 4.2.0).
(Heads up: Hexo 5 requires Node.js 10 or above.)
After updating, the flow is basically the same as in "
Learning Hexo from Scratch - Understanding the Hexo Directory Structure
hexo init blog # blog folder name, up to you
This part is the annoying bit. You can't just copy-paste the old
over — Hexo 5 changed a lot. I'd recommend using a diff tool to re-fill the values:
Plenty of diff tools online, pick whatever you're comfortable with.
(This bit is way more annoying than it should be, but if you want the latest Hexo, you're stuck doing it.)
What's different in Hexo 5 vs Hexo 4
Compile speed is faster — that's a given. If you're on the old 3.9.0 like me, updating to Hexo 4 or 5 makes a huge difference (especially if you have over a hundred posts).
For me personally, the biggest feature of Hexo 5 is that themes can finally be installed via
is available, why not use it?
On top of that, code highlighting now supports both highlight.js and Prism.js, so the feature set is much improved. You can read the official changelog for more:
There aren't huge differences in config, so I won't dwell on

Honestly, the update path is a pain — especially for themes. But Hexo 5 makes the theme update story much nicer. If you're on Hexo 3.9.0, I genuinely recommend updating to at least Hexo 4 — the compile speed difference is enormous.
